items.title: Dozens killed as Ukraine accuses Russia of breaking unilateral ceasefire
items.desc: The Russian strikes came just hours before a unilaterally proposed Ukrainian ceasefire and days before Russia itself intends to observe a ceasefire. Volodymyr Zelenskyy condemned "cynical, vile" attacks by Russia.

items.desc: If you were hoping to play Subnautica 2 on handhelds like the Steam Deck, you should be able to do so without much trouble at the Early Access release.Read the full article on GamingOnLinux.

items.title: ‘The rats are like a storm’: Palestinians seeking refuge in rubble struggle with rodent infestations
items.desc: Palestinians living in tents amid the rubble in the Gaza Strip and aid groups trying to help them say rodent infestations have left them suffering from diseases and unsure how to cope.

items.title: Canada to pick between Swedish and U.S. radar planes to protect its skies
items.desc: The government of Canada wants to quickly acquire half a dozen new "flying radars" — officially called Airborne Early Warning and Control (AEW&C) aircraft — at a cost of more than $5 billion.

items.title: Montrealers celebrate Canada's incoming GG Louise Arbour, who got her start in the city

items.title: North Korea drops reunification goal from constitution
items.desc: North Korea has rewritten its constitution to erase any goal of reunification with the South. The move locks in Pyongyang leader Kim Jong Un's shift toward treating Seoul as a permanent adversary.

items.title: Next Lifts Outlook as Strong Sales Offset Higher Iran Costs
items.desc: Next Plc raised its profit and sales outlook as the British fashion and homewares retailer saw robust demand at the start of the year, even as estimated costs related to the conflict in the Middle East tripled.

items.desc: Kyiv said on Wednesday that Russia had violated a unilateral ceasefire called by Ukraine by attacking Ukrainian cities with 108 combat drones and three missiles overnight. Russia had on Tuesday launched waves of drone, missile and bomb attacks on multiple cit…
